name: Don Sebesky
abstract: Donald John Sebesky (December 10, 1937 – April 29, 2023) was an American composer, arranger, conductor, and jazz trombonist. He was a multi-instrumentalist and could play a number of other instruments: keyboards, electric piano, organ, accordion, and clavinet.

concerts.label: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet
Midnight Performance: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet

works_created.t: You Must Remember This: A Cinematic Sing-Along

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Pugh and I were the two trombones and we played mostly baritone horn in that band... We’ve done a few albums since. I think I’ve done all of his albums with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I went back to New York, and we had a meeting, Don Sebesky, Creed, and I. Started discussing repertoire. That’s when we came up with that CD called Morning Star.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: that's when Don Sebesky came on. And Don Sebesky came on the group, I remember it was in the summertime because Don was out of school.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: Sebesky writes new chart for the band... Sebesky writes the charts
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Eddie Sauter) only responds to the interviewer's statements about Don Sebesky's opinions on Sauter's work. There is no evidence of direct personal interaction, collaboration, or mentorship between Sauter and Sebesky in the transcript.

about_quotes.speaker: Rudy Van Gelder
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Don Sebesky as a creative arranger Creed wanted to hire, but the interviewee (Rudy Van Gelder) does not mention any personal interaction, collaboration, or relationship with Don Sebesky in the provided text.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rudy Van Gelder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
